name: Marketing & Communication
description: Foundations of marketing and professional communication — understanding consumer behavior, the marketing mix, branding, advertising, digital marketing, and persuasive communication strategies.

modules.title: What Is Marketing?
modules.objectives: Define marketing and the marketing concept; trace the evolution of marketing from production to relationship era; distinguish marketing from advertising.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — What Is Marketing? (Career Content)
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business - Marketing #1: What Is Marketing?
modules.assignment: Write a 1-page reflection comparing two real-world brands: one that uses traditional marketing and one that uses modern relationship marketing. What is each brand selling beyond the product?

modules.title: Consumer Behavior
modules.objectives: Distinguish needs, wants, and demands; apply Maslow's hierarchy of needs to buying decisions; describe the consumer decision-making process; identify psychological factors influencing purchases.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — Consumer Behavior & Decision Making
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business - Marketing #2: Consumer Behavior
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — What Makes Something Memorable? (Psychology of Buying)
modules.assignment: Map out the five stages of the consumer decision-making process for a purchase you recently made. Identify which stage felt shortest and explain why.

modules.title: Market Segmentation & Targeting
modules.objectives: Identify the four bases of segmentation (demographic, psychographic, behavioral, geographic); evaluate potential target markets; explain the difference between mass marketing and niche marketing.
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business - Marketing #3: Market Segmentation
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — Why Brands Target Specific Groups
modules.assignment: Choose a consumer product (snack, app, clothing brand). Write a segmentation profile using all four bases and then define the single most profitable target segment with a rationale.

modules.title: The Marketing Mix: Product
modules.objectives: Classify products by type; describe the product life cycle stages and their marketing implications; explain branding and packaging decisions.
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — Why Do We Buy Things We Don't Need? (Packaging & Branding)
modules.assignment: Select a product and draw its product life cycle curve, labeling where you believe it currently sits. Recommend two marketing actions appropriate for that stage.

modules.title: The Marketing Mix: Price
modules.objectives: Compare cost-plus, competitive, and value-based pricing strategies; explain psychological pricing tactics; analyze how price communicates brand positioning.
modules.assignment: Research the pricing of three competing coffee brands (e.g., Starbucks, a local café, an instant brand). Identify which pricing strategy each uses and explain what that pricing signals about their brand positioning.

modules.title: The Marketing Mix: Place
modules.objectives: Map direct vs. indirect distribution channels; describe the roles in a supply chain; explain the growth of e-commerce as a distribution channel.
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business - Marketing #6: Place & Distribution
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — How Does Amazon Deliver So Fast? (Supply Chain)
modules.assignment: Trace the distribution channel for one physical product from raw material to the consumer. Draw a channel map and label each intermediary. Then compare with how a digital product (e.g., a song on Spotify) reaches the customer.

modules.title: The Marketing Mix: Promotion
modules.objectives: Identify the five elements of the promotional mix; explain integrated marketing communications (IMC); compare advertising, PR, personal selling, and sales promotion.
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business - Marketing #7: Promotion & IMC
modules.assignment: Find a brand campaign that uses at least three promotional mix elements simultaneously. Describe each element used, explain how they reinforce each other, and evaluate the campaign's effectiveness.

modules.title: Branding & Brand Identity
modules.objectives: Define brand equity and explain its value; analyze the components of brand identity (logo, voice, color, positioning); describe how brands build loyalty.
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — Why Is the Apple Logo So Powerful? (Brand Identity)
modules.assignment: Create a brand identity sheet for a fictional business: choose a name, logo concept, brand colors, tagline, tone of voice, and one positioning statement. Justify each choice in 2–3 sentences.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines marketing?
options: Selling products at the lowest possible price
Creating, communicating, delivering, and exchanging offerings that have value for customers
questions.answer: Creating, communicating, delivering, and exchanging offerings that have value for customers
questions.explanation: Marketing encompasses the full process of identifying, creating, and delivering value — not just advertising or selling.

questions.question: According to Maslow's hierarchy, which need must be satisfied before a consumer pursues esteem needs?
questions.explanation: Maslow's hierarchy orders needs: physiological → safety → social/belonging → esteem → self-actualization. Esteem comes after social needs.

questions.question: Segmenting a market by age, income, and gender is an example of which segmentation base?
questions.explanation: Demographic segmentation divides the market using population statistics such as age, gender, income, and education.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A product is in the maturity stage of its life cycle. Which strategy is most appropriate" is: Market penetration and product modifications to extend the life cycle.
questions.explanation: True. Market penetration and product modifications to extend the life cycle is correct.

questions.question: A company sells a product for $50. Production cost is $30. They use cost-plus pricing with a 20% markup on cost. What is the selling price?
questions.explanation: 20% markup on $30 cost = $6. Selling price = $30 + $6 = $36. (The $50 is a distractor.)

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a direct distribution channel?
questions.explanation: A direct channel has no intermediaries. The producer sells directly to the end consumer (e.g., a farm stand or a brand's own website).

questions.question: Integrated Marketing Communications (IMC) means:
options: Using only digital media to reach customers
Combining all promotional tools so they deliver a consistent message
Running advertisements and press releases at the same time
Integrating the marketing department with the finance department
questions.answer: Combining all promotional tools so they deliver a consistent message
questions.explanation: IMC coordinates advertising, PR, social media, personal selling, and other tools to present a unified brand message across all channels.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Pricing a product at $9.99 instead of $10.00 is an example of:" is: Value-based pricing.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Psychological pricing (charm pricing).

questions.question: Which stage of the consumer decision-making process involves comparing alternatives before purchasing?
questions.explanation: After gathering information, consumers weigh competing options against each other in the evaluation of alternatives stage.

questions.question: A company targeting only left-handed golfers is using which type of targeting strategy?
questions.explanation: Niche marketing focuses on a very specific, narrowly defined segment of the market.

questions.question: Which element of the promotional mix involves a company paying for media space to deliver its message?
questions.explanation: Advertising is paid, non-personal communication through mass media such as TV, radio, social media, and print.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which distribution channel is most common for fresh produce sold at a local farmers market" is: Producer → Consumer.
questions.explanation: True. Producer → Consumer is correct.

questions.question: A new energy drink brand is entering the market. Describe one appropriate pricing strategy they could use during the launch phase and explain why it fits that stage of the product life cycle.
questions.answer: Penetration pricing (low introductory price to gain market share quickly) or price skimming (high price targeting early adopters willing to pay a premium). Either is valid with appropriate justification for the introduction stage.
questions.explanation: During the introduction stage, a brand must either attract early adopters with a premium (skimming) or rapidly grow market share with a low price (penetration).

questions.question: Identify the four Ps of the marketing mix and give a one-sentence example of each for a smartphone brand.
questions.answer: Product (e.g., a high-spec phone with a premium camera); Price (e.g., $999 using value-based pricing); Place (e.g., sold through the brand's website and major electronics retailers); Promotion (e.g., a TV ad campaign and influencer partnerships on Instagram).
questions.explanation: The four Ps — Product, Price, Place, Promotion — form the core marketing mix that every brand must configure to reach its target market.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the five stages in the consumer decision-making process?
questions.explanation: The five stages are: problem recognition, information search, evaluation of alternatives, purchase decision, and post-purchase behavior. Advertising exposure is a marketing tool, not a stage.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which era of marketing history focused on selling what the factory produced, regardless of customer needs" is: Marketing era.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Production era.

questions.question: A teenager buys a brand-name sneaker not because it is more comfortable but because peers wear it. Which psychological factor best explains this?
questions.explanation: Reference groups (peers, celebrities) influence buying decisions by shaping what is seen as desirable or acceptable.

questions.question: Dividing a market based on lifestyle, values, and personality is called:
questions.explanation: Psychographic segmentation categorizes consumers by their lifestyle, values, attitudes, and personality traits.

questions.question: A product in the decline stage of its life cycle would most likely:
questions.explanation: In the decline stage, sales fall consistently. Companies typically cut marketing spending, harvest remaining profit, or discontinue/reposition the product.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Value-based pricing sets price based on:" is: How much the customer perceives the product is worth.
questions.explanation: True. How much the customer perceives the product is worth is correct.

questions.question: Which distribution channel element is responsible for physically moving goods from the producer to the customer?
questions.explanation: Logistics encompasses transportation, warehousing, and inventory management — the physical movement of goods through the supply chain.

questions.question: Which promotional mix element earns media coverage without paying for ad space?
questions.explanation: PR generates earned media — press coverage, reviews, and publicity that the brand does not pay for directly.

questions.question: Brand equity refers to:
options: The total assets on a brand's balance sheet
The added value a brand name gives to a product beyond its functional qualities
The cost of trademarking a brand logo
The number of product lines a company offers
questions.answer: The added value a brand name gives to a product beyond its functional qualities
questions.explanation: Brand equity is the premium a company earns because consumers associate positive perceptions with the brand name.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "SEO (Search Engine Optimization) is best described as:" is: Paying Google to display your ad at the top of search results.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Improving a website's organic ranking in search engine results.

questions.question: In the AIDA model, what does the 'D' stand for?
questions.explanation: AIDA = Attention, Interest, Desire, Action. After building interest, the ad must create desire — making the customer want the product.

questions.question: A press release is primarily a tool of:
questions.explanation: Press releases are written communications sent to journalists and media outlets — a core PR tactic for generating earned media coverage.

questions.question: Active listening in professional communication involves:
options: Waiting silently for your turn to speak
Focusing entirely on the speaker, asking clarifying questions, and paraphrasing back
Nodding frequently without engaging with the content
Interrupting to show that you are engaged
questions.answer: Focusing entirely on the speaker, asking clarifying questions, and paraphrasing back
questions.explanation: Active listening requires full attention, withholding judgment, reflecting back what you heard, and asking relevant questions.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "In rhetoric, 'logos' refers to persuasion through:" is: Logic, data, and reasoned argument.
questions.explanation: True. Logic, data, and reasoned argument is correct.

questions.question: A marketing campaign's KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) are used to:
options: Measure whether campaign objectives are being achieved
Choose which distribution channel to use
questions.answer: Measure whether campaign objectives are being achieved
questions.explanation: KPIs are quantifiable metrics (e.g., click-through rate, conversion rate, brand awareness lift) used to evaluate campaign performance against goals.

questions.question: Which social media marketing metric measures how often users interact with a post relative to how many saw it?
questions.explanation: Engagement rate = (likes + comments + shares) / reach × 100%. It measures the quality of audience interaction.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the difference between needs and wants?
options: Needs are expensive; wants are affordable
Needs are biological or psychological necessities; wants are desires shaped by culture and personality
Wants are more important than needs in marketing
Needs are created by advertising; wants are natural
questions.answer: Needs are biological or psychological necessities; wants are desires shaped by culture and personality
questions.explanation: A need is something required for survival or well-being (e.g., food). A want is a specific way to satisfy that need shaped by culture (e.g., sushi).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Content marketing is best defined as:" is: Paying influencers to endorse your product.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Creating and distributing valuable, relevant content to attract and retain a clearly defined audience.

questions.question: Explain what BATNA means in negotiation and why knowing your BATNA gives you leverage. Provide a real-life example.
questions.answer: BATNA = Best Alternative to a Negotiated Agreement. It is the best outcome you can achieve if the current negotiation fails. Knowing your BATNA prevents you from accepting an unfavorable deal and gives you confidence to walk away. Example: before negotiating a salary, if you have another offer for $55,000 you can confidently reject anything below that amount.
questions.explanation: BATNA is a core concept from Fisher & Ury's negotiation framework. The stronger your BATNA, the more leverage you have.

questions.question: You are launching a marketing campaign for a new plant-based burger. Briefly describe your target market using at least two segmentation bases, and explain which one promotional mix element you would prioritize and why.
questions.answer: Target market example: Ages 18–35 (demographic), health-conscious and environmentally aware (psychographic). Promotional element: social media marketing — because the target segment is active on Instagram and TikTok, content like recipe videos and influencer partnerships can reach them cost-effectively and drive engagement.
questions.explanation: This question tests integrated application of segmentation and the promotional mix — both are core course concepts.

questions.question: Describe two ways a company can respond to a PR crisis. Use a real brand example to support your answer.
questions.answer: Two strategies: (1) Acknowledge and apologize — take responsibility quickly and outline corrective actions (e.g., Johnson & Johnson's Tylenol recall in 1982, where they immediately pulled products and communicated transparently). (2) Deflect and minimize — dispute facts or shift blame (generally less effective, e.g., initial responses by United Airlines in 2017 before public pressure forced a proper apology). Transparency and swift action typically produce better long-term reputation outcomes.
questions.explanation: Crisis communication strategy directly affects brand equity and long-term consumer trust.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is the best definition of marketing?
options: Selling as many products as possible
Creating value and communicating it to customers
quizQuestions.answer: Creating value and communicating it to customers
quizQuestions.explanation: Marketing is about identifying and delivering value — not just selling or advertising.

quizQuestions.question: During which marketing era did companies focus mainly on production efficiency?
quizQuestions.explanation: The production era assumed demand would absorb whatever was efficiently produced.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is the best definition of marketing" is: Creating value and communicating it to customers.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Creating value and communicating it to customers is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "a company that surveys customers before developing a new product is practicing:."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Market orientation.

quizQuestions.question: According to Maslow, which need is at the base of the hierarchy?
quizQuestions.explanation: Physiological needs (food, water, shelter) are the most basic and must be met before higher needs motivate behavior.

quizQuestions.question: A 'want' in marketing is best described as:
options: A basic necessity required for survival
A specific desire shaped by culture and personality
A product that customers demand at a given price
A legal right that consumers possess
quizQuestions.answer: A specific desire shaped by culture and personality
quizQuestions.explanation: Wants are culturally and personally shaped forms of needs — e.g., needing food but wanting a specific cuisine.

quizQuestions.question: Which stage of the consumer decision-making process comes immediately after 'information search'?
quizQuestions.explanation: After gathering information, consumers compare options before making a purchase decision.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "According to Maslow, which need is at the base of the hierarchy" is: Physiological.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Physiological is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Dividing a market by region or climate is an example of:
quizQuestions.explanation: Geographic segmentation divides markets by location — country, region, city, climate zone, etc.

quizQuestions.question: Which segmentation base groups consumers by purchase frequency and brand loyalty?
quizQuestions.explanation: Behavioral segmentation groups buyers by usage rate, loyalty, purchase occasion, and benefits sought.

quizQuestions.question: A target market is:
options: Every person who might ever buy the product
The segment a company decides to focus its marketing efforts on
Customers who have already bought the product
A demographic profile of the general population
quizQuestions.answer: The segment a company decides to focus its marketing efforts on
quizQuestions.explanation: After segmenting and evaluating, a company selects one or more segments as its target market.

quizQuestions.question: During which stage of the product life cycle do sales grow rapidly and competitors begin to enter the market?
quizQuestions.explanation: The growth stage is marked by rapidly increasing sales, new competitors, and improving profit margins.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a convenience product?
quizQuestions.explanation: Convenience products are inexpensive, frequently purchased items bought with minimal effort.

quizQuestions.question: Branding provides value to consumers primarily by:
options: Reducing the price of the product
Helping consumers identify and trust a product's quality and origin
quizQuestions.answer: Helping consumers identify and trust a product's quality and origin
quizQuestions.explanation: Brands reduce consumer search costs and signal quality — strong brands command loyalty and premium pricing.

quizQuestions.question: A product's packaging serves which of the following marketing functions?
options: Protection, information, and attracting attention at point of purchase
quizQuestions.answer: Protection, information, and attracting attention at point of purchase
quizQuestions.explanation: Packaging protects the product, communicates information, and acts as a 'silent salesperson' on the shelf.

quizQuestions.question: Cost-plus pricing calculates price by:
options: Adding a fixed markup percentage to the cost of production
Setting price equal to perceived customer value
Charging the highest price the market will bear
quizQuestions.answer: Adding a fixed markup percentage to the cost of production
quizQuestions.explanation: Cost-plus pricing: price = cost + markup. Simple but ignores market demand and competition.

quizQuestions.question: Price skimming involves:
options: Setting a low initial price to capture market share quickly
Setting a high initial price targeting early adopters, then lowering it over time
quizQuestions.answer: Setting a high initial price targeting early adopters, then lowering it over time
quizQuestions.explanation: Skimming maximizes revenue from early adopters willing to pay a premium before lowering price for a broader audience.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Cost-plus pricing calculates price by:" is: Adding a fixed markup percentage to the cost of production.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Adding a fixed markup percentage to the cost of production is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Price skimming involves:" is: Setting a low initial price to capture market share quickly.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Setting a high initial price targeting early adopters, then lowering it over time.

quizQuestions.question: An intermediary that buys products in bulk from manufacturers and sells them to retailers is called a:
quizQuestions.explanation: Wholesalers buy large quantities from producers and redistribute in smaller lots to retailers.

quizQuestions.question: E-commerce has changed distribution primarily by:
options: Eliminating the need for product packaging
Enabling manufacturers to sell directly to consumers globally without physical stores
Making wholesalers more important than ever
quizQuestions.answer: Enabling manufacturers to sell directly to consumers globally without physical stores
quizQuestions.explanation: E-commerce creates a direct channel, bypassing traditional retail intermediaries and expanding geographic reach.

quizQuestions.question: In an indirect distribution channel, who typically sells the product directly to the end consumer?
quizQuestions.explanation: In most indirect channels, the retailer is the final link that sells to the consumer.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "An intermediary that buys products in bulk from manufacturers and sells them to retailers is called a:" is: Wholesaler.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Wholesaler is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The five elements of the promotional mix include advertising, PR, personal selling, sales promotion, and:
quizQuestions.explanation: The five elements of the promotional mix: advertising, PR, personal selling, sales promotion, and direct/digital marketing.

quizQuestions.question: A 'buy one get one free' offer is an example of:
quizQuestions.explanation: Sales promotions are short-term incentives (coupons, BOGO deals, contests) designed to stimulate immediate purchases.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The five elements of the promotional mix include advertising, PR, personal selling, sales promotion, and:" is: Direct / digital marketing.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Direct / digital marketing is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which promotional tool earns media coverage without paying for ad space."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Public relations.

quizQuestions.question: Brand equity is best defined as:
options: The total revenue a brand generates annually
The added value and premium customers associate with a brand name
The number of products a brand sells
The design cost of a company's logo
quizQuestions.answer: The added value and premium customers associate with a brand name
quizQuestions.explanation: High brand equity means consumers will choose and pay more for your product over an identical unbranded alternative.

quizQuestions.question: A brand's 'voice' refers to:
options: The celebrity spokesperson it uses in commercials
The consistent personality and tone a brand uses in all its communications
The volume of its advertising campaigns
The language the brand uses in foreign markets
quizQuestions.answer: The consistent personality and tone a brand uses in all its communications
quizQuestions.explanation: Brand voice is the distinct style, language, and personality a brand maintains across all touchpoints.

quizQuestions.question: Brand positioning describes:
options: Where a brand's products are sold
How a brand wants to be perceived in the minds of target consumers relative to competitors
The physical placement of a product on store shelves
A brand's pricing relative to competitors
quizQuestions.answer: How a brand wants to be perceived in the minds of target consumers relative to competitors
quizQuestions.explanation: Positioning is the mental space a brand occupies — the distinct perception it creates vs. alternatives.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Brand equity is best defined as:" is: The added value and premium customers associate with a brand name.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The added value and premium customers associate with a brand name is correct.
